Life Physics Reflection Philosophy Plans

Repost: Refelction after F=ma

February 14, 2026

So I took the F=ma like 2 days ago, here's the reflection: Before test: I basically studied as much as I could with sleeping 5-7 hrs, and that must have been the 10 days most locked in I have ever been in my life. I got so much homework and tests from school, and somehow managed to get in ~5 hrs of studying for physics every day. When I finished Hrk in early Feb, I started taking mocks and I saw a progression like 1 - 6 -10 - 12 raw score progression in mock performances, but it suddenly started decreasing and the mock I took day before going to f=ma was an 8 I think. I knew I was going to fail, so I didn't have much hopes and I didn't get much nervious.

On the test, I was feeling good in first 10 problems, after solving/educational guesses on about 7 of them. Then, on the rest of them, I tried but couldn't even approach the problem correctly. Essentially gussing with like 2 actual solves. Now looking at the answer sheet found online, I think I got like 10 correct and 5 problems I don't know if I got it right or not, and 10 problems which I got wrong.

Now that gives me a score of about 11 or 12? and that is not going to qualify me for usapho. I knew it was a gamble at first, and now that it has just failed, I don't know what to do. I'm applying to colleges soon, but I have no actual awards. No proof of the fact that I spent close to 800 hrs on math and physics competition lol.

I keep going back to thinking "what if I started earlier" kind of things. If I started physics a year earlier, I would have made usapho, and even if I got that extra month I wasted studying AIME, I would have been close to making the cutoff. If I studied AMC over the winter break I would have gotten an extra problem correct and made AIME. If I actually studied har,d I would have made AIME. If I didn't slack USAMTS, I would have gotten HM or even bronze.

I don't know if I'm not talented or anything, but I feel like I studied much more than an average AIME qualifier, and I deserve to qualify for AIME.

Also, the thing is, getting 93 on AMC 12 is soooo much different than getting 96 on AMC 12. The title AIME qualifier has so much meaning to it, and the fact that some people didn't decide to guess on 2 problems is so much valued than me guessing on 2 problems and getting them wrong. I think this actually made me hate taking risks, because my scioly robot tour path was purely designed so that no problems arise, and I shortened the bungee cord just so that it doens't it the floor.

Studying math and physics was genuinely fun, and I enjoyed doing them, but is it even any better than playing video games if it doens't help. I mean, yeah, all the studying does end up helping me in some ways, but I have to go to college, and I want to get into a good college. All the studying doesn't matter if there's no proper result from it.

Now, what I am going to try to do so that I can get into a good college:

Try to get into a good summer program. Last year, my plan during the summer was to get a job and study AMC, and I couldn't get a job (They all hated me). and I am going to write good essays to convince that I am actually qualified to go to those camps... hopefully.

SAT. I think getting 1550+ should be a good boost. Idk if I could, though. Time to read more philosophy books but idk if those help with SAT English. Philosophy books?

Scioly. Get like 5 podium finishes in 5 events in states comp. It should be light (it's not lol).

Essay comps. Idk but I like doing them and I did well last year so hoping for better results.

And maybe some personal projects.

Plans for oly events. I think I will try to get AIME qual. Can't really trust MAA but I think I can get qual without much prep..... Eitehr do F=ma again, or do USAAAO. It's all after college application deadline so it doesn't even matter. However, I want to make camp.... or get like a silver in an actual us olympiad.

Final analysis: I have gotten so unlucky so far and due to conservation of luck, I am going to end up getting lucky later in life.

Comments
